channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Treasury watchdog begins audit of Musk DOGE team's access to federal government's payment system
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Treasury Department's Office of Inspector General on Friday said it was launching an audit of the security controls for the federal government's payment system, after Democratic senators raised red flags about the access provided to Trump aide Elon Musk's Department of Governme
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge sets a 5-day deadline for the Trump administration to start lifting its USAID funding freeze
item.description: A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to temporarily lift a funding freeze shutting down U.S. humanitarian aid and development worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer And Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: McConnell tests the strengths and limits of his power opposing a trio of Trump's Cabinet nominees
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — No longer in charge, Sen. Mitch McConnell has been speaking his mind, the long-serving GOP leader rejecting President Donald Trump’s more high-profile Cabinet nominees — alone at times, among the Republicans, casting his no votes.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Abolish property taxes? DeSantis endorses the idea and explains how it could be done in Florida
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis endorsed the idea of abolishing property taxes in Florida, noting that it could be accomplished via a constitutional amendment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in Wisconsin's spring primary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Wisconsin’s top school official faces the first big hurdle of her reelection bid in the spring primary on Tuesday, when she’s running against two challengers in a far different political and electoral environment than she did in her successful 2021 campaign.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT YOON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Is Elon Musk overreaching with DOGE? Lawmakers debate
item.description: Is Elon Musk, head of DOGE, being transparent? Reps. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., and Byron Donalds, R-Fla., debate on NewsNation's "CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Hungary's Orbán predicts Trump will bring Russia back into Western fold after end of Ukraine war
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump's administration will reconnect Russia with the economies and energy networks of Western countries if the war in Ukraine, now nearly three years old, comes to an end, Hungary's nationalist prime minister said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Dem lawmakers answer USAID funding questions after bombshell report
item.description: Fox News Digital asked Democrat lawmakers their thoughts about controversial USAID funding, such as money ending up in the hands of terrorist-aligned groups.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge orders Trump administration to temporarily allow funds for foreign aid to flow again
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge ordered the Trump administration on Thursday to temporarily lift a three-week funding freeze that has shut down U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida passes immigration bill, repealing in-state tuition for undocumented students
item.description: The third and final day of the special session concluded with the passage of an immigration bill, one that sparked significant debate among lawmakers in both the House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Concerns over mass deportations lead to ‘Know Your Rights‘ discussion in Brooklyn Center
item.description: Amid concerns over the Trump Administration’s plans for mass deportations, nearly three dozen people who live in Brooklyn Park and Brooklyn Center attended a panel discussion Wednesday night to ask questions about how to respond if ICE shows up at a home or workplace, and also asked about the role o
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: At McMahon’s confirmation hearing, it was the Education Department on trial
item.description: In her confirmation hearing, Linda McMahon handled tough questions from Democrats, and affirmed President Trump’s plans to shrink, and potentially eliminate, the department.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner and Jonaki Mehta, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration was set to purchase $400 million worth of armored Teslas
item.description: That’s according to a public State Department procurement document. It comes as ethics experts raise conflict of interest questions about the chief executive of Tesla, Elon Musk, who is a top White House official.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Live updates: Trump, Modi talk trade and tariffs; Senate confirms Kennedy to lead HHS
item.description: President Trump welcomes his second global leader this week with a visit from Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota’s Tina Smith won't seek reelection to Senate
item.description: Sen. Tina Smith, who has represented Minnesota in the U.S. Senate since 2018, won’t try for another six-year term next year. An open seat could set off a scramble in both parties ahead of the midterm election.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, MPR News

item.title: Johnson leaves door open to full-year funding stopgap
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) didn’t rule out a full-year stopgap to keep the government funded through the end of the fiscal year, as lawmakers struggle to strike a bipartisan deal. Pressed by reporters on the chances of a full-year stopgap, Johnson left the door open on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ sues NY Gov. Hochul, AG James over immigration policies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's newly installed attorney general, Pam Bondi, went after New York leaders Wednesday over the state's immigration policies, announcing a lawsuit in the latest effort by the Republican administration to carry out the president's hardline immigration campaign p
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Judge won't halt Trump, Musk federal worker buyout program
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge George O’Toole found that the unions who had sued over the directive did not have standing to do so.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard awaits Senate vote on director of national intelligence nomination
item.description: Her chances of confirmation increased when Republicans fell in line following a pressure campaign by Trump allies.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fogel release a sign of Trump, Putin's 'great friendship': Witkoff
item.description: Trump and Putin had a "great friendship, and I think now it's going to continue," special envoy Steve Witkoff said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump official targeting Jan. 6 investigators worked on those cases himself
item.description: The disconnect between Bove's aggressive stance at the time to hold rioters accountable — and his current hostility towards the Jan. 6 probe — has troubled some former colleagues.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Egg prices unite Nevada lawmakers as AB171 makes its way toward approval
item.description: Nevada lawmakers continued Tuesday to fast-track legislation that could bring down the price of eggs, pushing Assembly Bill 171 (AB171) closer to law.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ukraine selling arms to cartels? Tucker Carlson claims rebutted
item.description: Carlson claims U.S. intelligence agencies know about the billions in transactions to arm “our actual enemies.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: It’s the last week to change party affiliation ahead of NYC mayor’s race
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Friday is the last day to change your party registration ahead of the 2025 mayoral election. In June, New York City voters will pick their party’s candidate for the mayoral election in November.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Battleground Florida: Officials discuss tariffs, LGBTQ policies
item.description: University of Tampa Economics Professor Michael Coon explained the purpose of tariffs and the impact they will likely have on businesses and families in Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump fires Office of Government Ethics chief
item.description: President Trump has fired the director of the Office of Government Ethics, the agency announced Monday. The office posted on its website that it had been notified Trump was removing David Huitema, who had been nominated by former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: HIV infections could jump over 6 times if US support dropped & not replaced, UNAIDS chief says
item.description: The head of the U.N. AIDS agency said Monday the number of new HIV infections could jump more than six times by 2029 if American support of the biggest AIDS program is dropped, warning that millions of people could die and more resistant strains of the disease could emerge.
item.dc:creator: MARIA CHENG AP Medical Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP senators 'terrified' of crossing Trump, facing Musk-funded challengers
item.description: GOP senators are "terrified" over the prospect of facing primary challengers funded by Elon Musk if they stick their necks out by opposing President Donald Trump's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tells Treasury Department to stop making pennies
item.description: President Trump claims he has instructed the U.S. Treasury Department to stop producing pennies, calling the 1-cent coin wasteful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Members of Congress denied access to Department of Education
item.description: Multiple members of Congress were denied entry to the Department of Education on Friday, according to videos and social media posts from lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
